skill.name: specdd-refactor
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to refactor code, tests, docs, specs, or project structure while preserving active SpecDD behavior.
skill.summary: Refactor code, tests, docs, and project structure in SpecDD projects without changing specified behavior.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: All 10 static analysis findings are false positives. The 7 external_commands alerts were triggered by Markdown inline code backticks (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`), not shell execution. The 3 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings were caused by the license identifier 'Apache-2.0' and the filename 'bootstrap.md' being misidentified as crypto terms. This skill is a pure documentation and methodology guide with no executable code, no network access, no env access, and no real shell commands. No prompt injection detected. Safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 7 instances of backtick characters as Ruby shell execution. All backticks are Markdown inline code formatting for file paths and bootstrap file names. No actual shell commands are present.

low_findings.title: License and filename misidentified as cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Apache-2.0' on line 3 and 'bootstrap.md' on lines 54 and 58 as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are an SPDX license identifier and a Markdown filename. No cryptography is present in this skill.

content.user_title: Refactor code safely in SpecDD projects
content.value_statement: Refactoring in spec-driven projects risks silently changing contract behavior. This skill guides structural changes that preserve the behavior defined by active SpecDD specs while keeping write authority intact.
actual_capabilities: Refactor code, tests, docs, and project structure while preserving specified behavior
Resolve the active SpecDD bootstrap chain in correct priority order
Identify the effective spec chain for any target before editing
Confirm write authority for every file before moving or changing it
Apply small mechanical refactoring steps with verification between risky changes
Stop and escalate to behavior-change authority when a refactor must alter specs
limitations: Requires an existing SpecDD project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Cannot infer write authority from filenames or conventions without spec definitions
Will not rename public symbols or widen dependencies outside the explicit refactor scope
Cannot adopt SpecDD in a non-SpecDD project (use specdd-adopt instead)

use_cases.title: Restructure a module without changing behavior
use_cases.description: A developer wants to reorganize files and folders in a SpecDD module. The skill resolves the spec chain, confirms write authority, and applies small mechanical steps that preserve all contracts.

use_cases.title: Refactor tests to match new structure
use_cases.description: A team moves test files to a new layout after restructuring source code. The skill ensures test behavior matches active spec contracts and runs baseline checks before and after.

use_cases.title: Clean up project structure during maintenance
use_cases.description: A tech lead schedules a maintenance window for structural cleanup. The skill guides a safe refactor workflow that respects spec boundaries and prevents silent contract changes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a single file within spec bounds
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor the file at [file_path] to [desired_structure]. Resolve the spec chain for this target using the active bootstrap. Confirm write authority, then apply small mechanical steps that preserve all specified behavior.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs to restructure a single file without changing its contract

prompt_templates.title: Move files across directories safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Move the files listed at [file_list] from [source_dir] to [target_dir]. Resolve the spec chain for each file, confirm write authority, and update all spec references that point to the old paths.
prompt_templates.scenario: A team needs to reorganize project layout while keeping specs accurate

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a module with verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor the module at [module_path] to [desired_structure]. Run baseline checks before starting, apply the refactor in small steps, and rerun checks after each step. Report any step that fails verification.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs a verified refactor workflow for a complex module

prompt_templates.title: Full project structural refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a structural refactor of the project at [project_root] following the plan at [refactor_plan]. Resolve all relevant spec chains, confirm write authority for every change, run baseline checks, and produce a change report at the end.
prompt_templates.scenario: A tech lead needs a complete, verified structural refactor of a project

output_examples.input: Refactor src/payments/ to use a new service-layer pattern. Preserve all behavior defined in the Payments spec.
output: A summary of the spec chain resolved for src/payments/ including governing specs and write authority sources.
A step-by-step refactor plan with verification checkpoints between each step.
A change report listing every file moved or modified, with a mapping to the spec contract that authorized each change.

output_examples.input: Move all test files from tests/legacy/ to tests/v2/. Update all references in specs and docs.
output: The spec chain for tests/legacy/ was resolved, confirming write authority for both the move and the spec reference updates.
All test files were moved to tests/v2/ and every spec and doc reference to the old path was updated.
Baseline test suite was run before and after the move. Results match. No behavior was changed.

best_practices: Always resolve the full bootstrap chain and confirm write authority before making any structural change
Apply refactors in small mechanical steps with verification between each risky change
Run baseline checks before and after refactoring to confirm behavior is preserved
anti_patterns: Moving files or responsibility across spec boundaries without checking that the specs authorize the change
Renaming public symbols, paths, or contracts as part of a refactor unless explicitly in scope
Treating a behavior change as a refactor and proceeding without resolving authority for the new behavior

faq.question: What does SpecDD mean?
faq.answer: SpecDD is a specification-driven development methodology where contracts and specs are treated as source-adjacent artifacts that govern implementation, tests, and documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill work with non-SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires an existing SpecDD project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file. Use the specdd-adopt skill to add SpecDD to a new project.

faq.question: Can this skill change specified behavior?
faq.answer: No. A refactor must preserve behavior defined by active specs. If a change must alter behavior, this skill stops and the change is resolved as a separate behavior-change task with its own authority.

faq.question: Does this skill use external CLI tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill operates entirely through file reading, writing, and standard test or build commands invoked by the operator. It does not define any shell execution of its own.

faq.question: How does the skill handle conflicting specs?
faq.answer: The skill reads bootstrap files in a defined priority order and applies them sequentially. Later files narrow earlier rules but must not silently weaken project contracts or write authority.

faq.question: What happens when write authority is unclear?
faq.answer: The skill stops and reports the missing authority. It will not move files or rename symbols based on filename patterns or conventions alone.
